<findings>

[observation] [info] The view/component split (analysis.ts as page, the remaining files as components) is a clean separation of composition from implementation within this feature zone.
[observation] [warning] analyze-panel.ts and proposal-editor.ts lack unit tests while the simpler smart-add-input and batch-import-panel components are tested — the more complex components should be prioritized for test coverage.
[pattern] [info] prd-analysis-panels is a strict terminal node in the call graph (0 incoming, 3 outgoing). The bidirectional import relationship with web-dashboard noted in the zone-level cross-imports does not produce bidirectional call-graph edges — the reverse direction carries only type imports. No circular runtime dependency exists.
[anti-pattern] [warning] Proposal, ProposalFeature, and ProposalTask types are defined locally in analyze-panel.ts rather than imported from a shared rex schema or gateway. The local copy will silently diverge from the API response shape if the server-side PRD proposal format changes.
[anti-pattern] [warning] `useState(() => { loadPending(); })` in analyze-panel.ts misuses the useState initializer to trigger an async network call. This is not the intended use of the initializer (which sets initial synchronous state) and bypasses the standard effect lifecycle, making cleanup and double-invocation behavior undefined.
[suggestion] [warning] The fix for the useState misuse in analyze-panel.ts requires two coordinated edits: (1) add useEffect to the import on line 9: `import { useState, useCallback, useEffect } from 'preact/hooks'`, and (2) replace line 74 with `useEffect(() => { loadPending(); }, [])`. Both changes must land together — changing line 74 without updating the import will fail the TypeScript build.
[suggestion] [info] proposal-editor.ts and smart-add-input.ts do not follow the *-panel.ts naming convention used by analyze-panel.ts and batch-import-panel.ts. Renaming to proposal-editor-panel.ts and smart-add-input-panel.ts (or adopting a consistent *-component.ts suffix across all four) would unify the naming scheme within the zone.

</findings>

<insights>

- The bidirectional import relationship with web-dashboard (2 imports each way) is expected for a feature-level UI cluster; ensure that panel components do not import from each other in ways that create rendering cycles.
- smart-add-input.ts and batch-import-panel.ts both have unit tests in this zone, but analyze-panel.ts and proposal-editor.ts do not — gap in test coverage for the more complex panel components.
- analysis.ts acting as a page-level entrypoint that composes the panel components is a clean architectural pattern; it is the correct aggregation point for this feature slice.
- analyze-panel.ts and proposal-editor.ts lack unit tests while the simpler smart-add-input and batch-import-panel components are tested — the more complex components should be prioritized for test coverage.
- The view/component split (analysis.ts as page, the remaining files as components) is a clean separation of composition from implementation within this feature zone.
- Call graph shows 0 incoming edges — no other zone calls into prd-analysis-panels at runtime. It is a pure terminal consumer: it receives data and events from web-viewer but nothing depends on it. This is the expected topology for a feature UI leaf.
- prd-analysis-panels is a strict terminal node in the call graph (0 incoming, 3 outgoing). The bidirectional import relationship with web-dashboard noted in the zone-level cross-imports does not produce bidirectional call-graph edges — the reverse direction carries only type imports. No circular runtime dependency exists.
- `analyze-panel.ts` line 74 calls `useState(() => { loadPending(); })` to trigger an async side effect on first render. `useState`'s initializer is only called once and is not designed for async operations — under React/Preact strict mode double-invocation, the async call would fire twice without cleanup. This should use `useEffect` with an empty dependency array instead.
- analyze-panel.ts directly encodes the Proposal/ProposalFeature/ProposalTask type hierarchy as local interfaces rather than importing them from a shared schema or rex-gateway. If the server-side response shape changes, this component's local types will silently drift from the API.
- `useState(() => { loadPending(); })` in analyze-panel.ts misuses the useState initializer to trigger an async network call. This is not the intended use of the initializer (which sets initial synchronous state) and bypasses the standard effect lifecycle, making cleanup and double-invocation behavior undefined.
- Proposal, ProposalFeature, and ProposalTask types are defined locally in analyze-panel.ts rather than imported from a shared rex schema or gateway. The local copy will silently diverge from the API response shape if the server-side PRD proposal format changes.
- useEffect is entirely absent from analyze-panel.ts's import list — the file imports useState and useCallback but never useEffect. The useState(() => { loadPending(); }) misuse on line 74 is therefore a consequence of a missing import, not a style preference. Fixing it requires two coordinated changes: add useEffect to the import on line 9 and replace line 74 with useEffect(() => { loadPending(); }, []).
- Naming inconsistency within the zone: analyze-panel.ts and batch-import-panel.ts follow a *-panel.ts suffix matching the zone's 'prd-analysis-panels' identity, but proposal-editor.ts and smart-add-input.ts do not follow this pattern — two different component-naming conventions coexist in the same 7-file zone.
- The fix for the useState misuse in analyze-panel.ts requires two coordinated edits: (1) add useEffect to the import on line 9: `import { useState, useCallback, useEffect } from 'preact/hooks'`, and (2) replace line 74 with `useEffect(() => { loadPending(); }, [])`. Both changes must land together — changing line 74 without updating the import will fail the TypeScript build.
- proposal-editor.ts and smart-add-input.ts do not follow the *-panel.ts naming convention used by analyze-panel.ts and batch-import-panel.ts. Renaming to proposal-editor-panel.ts and smart-add-input-panel.ts (or adopting a consistent *-component.ts suffix across all four) would unify the naming scheme within the zone.
- [call graph] 170 internal calls, 3 outgoing, 0 incoming (cohesion: 0.98, coupling: 0.02)

</insights>
